args.query: SELECT book_id, title, author, rating_number, categories, details FROM books_info WHERE store = 'Amazon' AND (categories ILIKE '%Literature & Fiction%' OR details ILIKE '%Literature & Fiction%') AND (details ILIKE '%English%' OR categories ILIKE '%English%');

exec_args.sql: SELECT book_id, title, author, rating_number, categories, details FROM books_info WHERE store = 'Amazon' AND (categories ILIKE '%Literature & Fiction%' OR details ILIKE '%Literature & Fiction%') AND (details ILIKE '%English%' OR categories ILIKE '%English%');

args.query: SELECT book_id, title, author, rating_number, categories, details FROM books_info;

exec_args.sql: SELECT book_id, title, author, rating_number, categories, details FROM books_info;

args.code:
import json, pandas as pd
perfect = pd.DataFrame(var_call_KP6qknN95FrTZH9cCygaOeLa)
with open(var_call_BaefRh6sPr7PJKu9cZYBTxET, 'r') as f:
    books = pd.DataFrame(json.load(f))
# filter books for English + Literature & Fiction
mask = books['categories'].str.contains('Literature & Fiction', case=False, na=False) | books['details'].str.contains('Literature & Fiction', case=False, na=False)
mask_eng = books['details'].str.contains('English', case=False, na=False) | books['categories'].str.contains('English', case=False, na=False)
books_filt = books[mask & mask_eng]
# join on exact id equality between purchase_id and book_id (hint mentioned fuzzy, but ids seem aligned)
merged = perfect.merge(books_filt, left_on='purchase_id', right_on='book_id', how='inner')
result = merged[['book_id','title','author','rating_number','categories','details']].to_dict(orient='records')
res_json = json.dumps(result)
print('__RESULT__:')
print(res_json)

val_args.code:
import json, pandas as pd
perfect = pd.DataFrame(var_call_KP6qknN95FrTZH9cCygaOeLa)
with open(var_call_BaefRh6sPr7PJKu9cZYBTxET, 'r') as f:
    books = pd.DataFrame(json.load(f))
# filter books for English + Literature & Fiction
mask = books['categories'].str.contains('Literature & Fiction', case=False, na=False) | books['details'].str.contains('Literature & Fiction', case=False, na=False)
mask_eng = books['details'].str.contains('English', case=False, na=False) | books['categories'].str.contains('English', case=False, na=False)
books_filt = books[mask & mask_eng]
# join on exact id equality between purchase_id and book_id (hint mentioned fuzzy, but ids seem aligned)
merged = perfect.merge(books_filt, left_on='purchase_id', right_on='book_id', how='inner')
result = merged[['book_id','title','author','rating_number','categories','details']].to_dict(orient='records')
res_json = json.dumps(result)
print('__RESULT__:')
print(res_json)
